THE NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE IN RALEIGH NC HAS CONFIRMED THAT A TORNADO BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN APPROXIMATELY 10 MILES NORTHWEST OF ROXBORO...PERSON COUNTY NORTH CAROLINA AROUND 1225 PM EDT ON JULY 2 2013. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR APPROXIMATELY 2 MINUTES. DURING THAT TIME...SIGNIFICANT TREE AND STRUCTURE DAMAGE TO SEVERAL HOMES AND COVERED BOAT DOCKS ON HYCO LAKE HAD OCCURRED. THE TORNADO HAS BEEN PRELIMINARILY RATED AN EF0 WITH MAXIMUM WIND SPEEDS OF 80-85 MPH.

DOUBLE WIDE HOME ON BROOKSTONE ROAD WAS PUSHED SEVERAL INCHES OFF ITS CINDER BLOCK FOUNDATION AND THE UNANCHORED ROOF WAS BLOWN OFF. SEVERAL SHINGLES WERE BLOWN OFF OF AN ADJACENT HOME...AND A HOLLOW OAK TREE WAS DOWNED NEARBY. THE DAMAGE WAS A RESULT OF STRAIGHT LINE WINDS AND OCCURRED BETWEEN 650-655 PM BASED ON THE 911 CALL TIME AND RADAR IMAGERY.
